title_en: The Quranic Command to Send Blessings
content_en:
The command to send blessings upon the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) — known as salawat or salah 'ala an-Nabi — has its ultimate foundation in one of the most theologically rich and most personally affecting verses in the entire Quran: 'Indeed, Allah and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet. O you who have believed, ask [Allah to confer] blessing upon him and ask [Allah to grant him] peace' (33:56). This verse, known among scholars as ayat al-salawat, is extraordinary in the cosmological vision it presents and in the imperative it places upon every believing man and woman.

Ismail ibn Ishaq al-Jahdhami's classical work on the virtues of sending blessings on the Prophet — one of the first comprehensive treatments of this subject in the Islamic scholarly tradition — begins with a careful examination of this Quranic verse. Its theological implications are profound. The verse reveals that Allah Himself, the Creator and Sustainer of all existence, sends blessings (salah) upon His Prophet. The nature of this divine salah is understood by the scholars as the divine praise, elevation, and honoring of the Prophet — the divine declaration of his excellence and the divine bestowal of mercy and honor upon him. This divine act of blessing the Prophet is continuous and eternal, reflecting the incomparable status that the Prophet Muhammad holds in the divine estimation.

The angels, who are the noblest of all created beings, also send blessings upon the Prophet — and they do so continually, as part of their ongoing worship and praise of Allah. The image presented by this verse is one of a universal chorus of divine and angelic honor directed toward the Prophet — the entire upper spiritual realm engaged in continuous praise and blessing of the man who brought Allah's final message to humanity. Against this backdrop of divine and angelic salawat, the command to the believers to also send blessings upon the Prophet represents an invitation to join this cosmic celebration — to add the human voice to the divine and angelic chorus of praise.

The word used in the Quran for the believers' salawat is different from that used for Allah's: the believers' salawat is a supplication — a request to Allah to send His blessings upon the Prophet — rather than a direct act. This distinction is theologically significant: only Allah can truly bless and honor the Prophet in the ultimate sense; what the believers are commanded to do is to ask Allah to do so, expressing thereby their love for the Prophet, their acknowledgment of his supreme status, and their desire to participate in the cosmic honoring of the Final Messenger.

The command 'O you who have believed' — using the Arabic form of address that the Quran employs for its most important injunctions to the Muslim community — places the salawat among the divinely mandated religious obligations of the believer. The scholars have discussed whether the salawat is obligatory at specific times (during the prayer, upon mention of the Prophet's name, and in specific other contexts) or more broadly mandated as a constant practice. What is universally agreed upon is that the salawat is a divinely commanded act of worship whose performance earns extraordinary divine reward and whose neglect is a form of religious shortcoming.

Al-Jahdhami's work establishes the salawat's Quranic foundation with scholarly depth and then proceeds to build upon it an extensive examination of the virtues, conditions, and formulas of sending blessings on the Prophet — creating the most comprehensive early treatment of this beautiful Islamic practice available in the classical tradition.
content_id:
Perintah untuk membaca shalawat kepada Nabi Muhammad ﷺ — yang dikenal sebagai shalawat atau salah 'ala an-Nabi — memiliki landasan utamanya dalam salah satu ayat Al-Quran yang paling kaya secara teologis dan paling menyentuh hati: "Sesungguhnya Allah dan para malaikat-Nya bershalawat untuk Nabi. Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, bershalawatlah kamu untuk Nabi dan ucapkanlah salam penghormatan kepadanya" (33:56). Ayat ini, yang dikenal di kalangan ulama sebagai ayat al-shalawat, sungguh luar biasa dalam visi kosmologis yang dihadirkannya dan dalam kewajiban yang diletakkannya kepada setiap mukmin laki-laki dan perempuan.

Karya klasik Ismail ibn Ishaq al-Jahdhami tentang keutamaan membaca shalawat kepada Nabi — salah satu pembahasan komprehensif pertama mengenai subjek ini dalam tradisi keilmuan Islam — dimulai dengan kajian mendalam atas ayat Al-Quran ini. Implikasi teologisnya sangat dalam. Ayat tersebut mengungkapkan bahwa Allah sendiri, Sang Pencipta dan Pemelihara seluruh alam, bershalawat kepada Nabi-Nya. Hakikat shalawat ilahi ini dipahami oleh para ulama sebagai pujian, peninggian derajat, dan pengmuliaan Allah terhadap Nabi — pernyataan ilahi tentang keunggulannya dan pemberian rahmat serta kehormatan dari Allah kepadanya. Tindakan ilahi ini dalam memberkati Nabi bersifat terus-menerus dan abadi, mencerminkan kedudukan Nabi Muhammad yang tiada bandingnya di sisi Allah.

Para malaikat, yang merupakan makhluk paling mulia di antara seluruh ciptaan, juga bershalawat kepada Nabi — dan mereka melakukannya secara terus-menerus, sebagai bagian dari ibadah dan pujian mereka yang tiada henti kepada Allah. Gambaran yang disajikan ayat ini adalah paduan suara universal dari penghormatan ilahi dan malaikat yang ditujukan kepada Nabi — seluruh alam spiritual tertinggi terlibat dalam pujian dan shalawat yang tak henti-hentinya kepada manusia yang membawa pesan terakhir Allah kepada umat manusia. Dengan latar belakang shalawat ilahi dan malaikat ini, perintah kepada kaum beriman untuk turut membaca shalawat kepada Nabi merupakan undangan untuk bergabung dalam perayaan kosmik ini — untuk menambahkan suara manusia ke dalam paduan suara pujian ilahi dan malaikat.

Kata yang digunakan Al-Quran untuk shalawat kaum beriman berbeda dari kata yang digunakan untuk shalawat Allah: shalawat kaum beriman adalah doa — permohonan kepada Allah agar mencurahkan berkah-Nya kepada Nabi — bukan tindakan langsung. Perbedaan ini memiliki signifikansi teologis: hanya Allah yang dapat sungguh-sungguh memberkati dan memuliakan Nabi dalam pengertian tertinggi; yang diperintahkan kepada kaum beriman adalah memohon kepada Allah untuk melakukannya, sehingga mengungkapkan kecintaan mereka kepada Nabi, pengakuan mereka atas kedudukannya yang tertinggi, dan keinginan mereka untuk berpartisipasi dalam pengmuliaan kosmik terhadap Rasul terakhir.

Perintah "Wahai orang-orang yang beriman" — menggunakan bentuk seruan bahasa Arab yang dipakai Al-Quran untuk kewajiban-kewajiban terpentingnya kepada umat Muslim — menempatkan shalawat di antara kewajiban agama yang ditetapkan Allah bagi orang beriman. Para ulama telah membahas apakah shalawat itu wajib pada waktu-waktu tertentu (dalam shalat, ketika nama Nabi disebut, dan dalam konteks tertentu lainnya) atau lebih luas diwajibkan sebagai amalan yang terus-menerus. Yang disepakati secara universal adalah bahwa shalawat merupakan ibadah yang diperintahkan Allah, yang pelaksanaannya mendatangkan pahala ilahi yang luar biasa, dan pengabaiannya merupakan bentuk kekurangan dalam beragama.

Karya al-Jahdhami menegakkan landasan Qurani dari shalawat dengan kedalaman ilmiah, kemudian melanjutkannya dengan kajian ekstensif tentang keutamaan, syarat-syarat, dan redaksi-redaksi shalawat kepada Nabi — menciptakan pembahasan awal paling komprehensif tentang amalan Islam yang indah ini yang tersedia dalam tradisi klasik.
content_ar: The command to send البركات upon النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ (ﷺ) — known as salawat or الصلاة 'ala an-Nabi — has its ultimate foundation in one of the most theologically rich and most personally affecting الآيات in the entire القرآن الكريم: 'Indeed, الله and His الملائكة send البركات upon النبي ﷺ. O you who have believed, ask [الله to confer] البركة upon him and ask [الله to grant him] peace' (33:56). This الآية, known among العلماء as ayat al-salawat, is extraordinary in the cosmological vision it presents and in the imperative it places upon every believing man and woman. Ismail ibn Ishaq al-Jahdhami's الكلاسيكي work on the الفضائل of sending البركات on النبي ﷺ — one of the أولاً comprehensive treatments of this subject in the Islamic العلمي التراث — begins with a careful examination of this القرآني الآية. Its theological implications are profound. The الآية reveals that الله Himself, the Creator and Sustainer of all existence, sends البركات (الصلاة) upon His النبي. The nature of this divine الصلاة is understood by the العلماء as the divine praise, elevation, and honoring of النبي ﷺ — the divine declaration of his excellence and the divine bestowal of الرحمة and الشرف upon him. This divine act of البركة النبي ﷺ is continuous and eternal, reflecting the incomparable status that النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ holds in the divine estimation. The الملائكة, who are the noblest of all created beings, أيضاً send البركات upon النبي ﷺ — and they do لذا continually, as part of their ongoing worship and praise of الله. The image presented by this الآية is one of a universal chorus of divine and angelic الشرف directed toward النبي ﷺ — the entire upper spiritual realm engaged in continuous praise and البركة of the man who brought الله's final message to humanity. Against this backdrop of divine and angelic salawat, the command to the المؤمنون to أيضاً send البركات upon النبي ﷺ represents an invitation to join this cosmic celebration — to add the human voice to the divine and angelic chorus of praise. The word used in the القرآن الكريم for the المؤمنون' salawat is different from that used for الله's: the المؤمنون' salawat is a الدعاء — a request to الله to send His البركات upon النبي ﷺ — rather than a direct act. This distinction is theologically المهم: only الله can truly bless and الشرف النبي ﷺ in the ultimate sense; what the المؤمنون are commanded to do is to ask الله to do لذا, expressing thereby their المحبة for النبي ﷺ, their acknowledgment of his supreme status, and their desire to participate in the cosmic honoring of the Final Messenger. The command 'O you who have believed' — using the Arabic form of address that the القرآن الكريم employs for its most المهم injunctions to the مسلم الجماعة — places the salawat among the divinely mandated religious obligations of the المؤمن. The العلماء have discussed whether the salawat is obligatory at specific times
